key_insight: Parallel Shapley assigns reward to each reasoning path by estimating its marginal contribution, instead of giving all paths the same outcome-level signal.
engineering_relevance: This makes multi-path RL training for LLM reasoning more stable, interpretable, and better suited for filtering redundant or harmful branches.
method: Treat each reasoning path as a player in a cooperative game.
Use a generative reward model to score path utilities.
Approximate Shapley values with Monte Carlo sampling for efficient path-level reward attribution.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports improved performance and more stable training on mathematical reasoning benchmarks compared with existing baselines.
watch_item: Shapley estimation via Monte Carlo and reward-model scoring may add compute overhead and depend on the quality of the reward model.
paper_title: Fishing Out Free Riders: Shapley-Based Reward Attribution for Parallel Reasoning via Reinforcement Learning
